premise_examined: two close, near-parallel passes through a Cheb-r ball (r<=10) force a (near-)collinear triple, which triple-freeness forbids => bounded pass count A0
verdict: REFUTED. Triple-freeness gives NO bound on the pass count. Near-parallel is not forbidden; only EXACT collinearity (cross=0) is, and parallel-but-OFFSET lattice arcs never produce a zero cross product.

decisive_measurements_L6_L7.note_cross: |cross((a2-a1),(b-a1))| bottoms out at 1 (the SMALLEST nonzero integer) for close near-parallel passes at every rho. Triple-freeness (cross != 0) is satisfied with the thinnest possible margin and imposes no angular gap.

close_and_near_parallel_coexisting_pairs.meaning: the supposed FORBIDDEN region (close AND near-parallel) is heavily POPULATED - not empty.

why_it_fails_geometrically: Two lattice arcs can be exactly parallel (angle 0) and Chebyshev-close and still triple-free, because they are laterally OFFSET: no point of one is collinear with any two of the other (cross = 1, not 0). Triple-freeness = distinct primitive chord directions from an anchor = O(r^3) directions of sup-norm <=2r (geomcap), loose by ~84x vs the measured A0<=5, and it CANNOT be sharpened by angle/separation because the offending configuration (parallel offset passes) is legal.
kappa3_locally_vacuous: confirmed by prior RESULTS.json: local kappa_3 <= 4 at rho=10 (magnitude ceiling floor(log3(8r^2))=6); the global kappa_3<=12 needs chord Cheb-distance ~515, far outside r<=10. Provides ZERO local constraint. No Diophantine direction-packing bound tightens A0.
A0_from_triple_free_alone: O(r^3) (geomcap(2r): 144/1008/14256 at r=2/4/10). NOT level-independent A0<=5. The measured flat A0<=5 is METRIC (M-contraction/twist/deep-tail separation), a DIFFERENT exclusion force than triple-freeness.
consistency_with_prior_route1: Matches design/lemma/route1/RESULTS.json (STUCK) and confirms it with the pass-level self-exclusion test the reframing asked for. The RETURNS-CONCLUSION.json 'CLOSES-MODULO-CLEAN-LEMMA' route does NOT use triple-freeness - it telescopes A(r) through 4 M-levels down to the single-level metric refill/anti-stacking lemma. That refill lemma is the real content; triple-freeness is not the exclusion force.
status: STUCK (for the triple-free self-exclusion mechanism specifically)
scripts: design/lemma/route1/selfexcl-results.json  (full per-rho pair tables + densest-ball dumps)
